what_it_does: Renders the 'Vibe' workspace: a project starting board that displays the project concept, style-anchor references, loose ideas, a chosen direction/mood, a material palette, and a list of what carries forward into downstream generation. It also registers the current workspace and active sequence in a global Vi context on mount.
observations: Filters mockups by `status === 'style_anchor'` to show reference anchors
Hardcodes a `mood` object and `ideas` array as local data
Sets `window.ViContext` inside a `useEffectVibe` with empty dependency array
Uses a mix of CSS utility classes and inline styles for layout
Renders a 'carry forward' panel listing style anchors, anti-pattern memory, palette, and QA rules
Includes a `CarryRow` helper component for icon+label+detail rows
Uses `Btn`, `Panel`, `Chip`, `MockThumb`, `Icon`, and `ViNote` components assumed available globally
standouts: The 'Vibe' workspace is framed as a warm, physical mood board rather than a form
Explicit 'What carries forward' section makes the downstream generation contract visible
Vi note explains that anchors, palette, and anti-patterns ride along automatically
Style anchors are treated as persistent context for every generation
conventions: Functional React component with props destructuring
Inline styles combined with CSS utility classes
Global window-based runtime data and component registry
Index-based keys for static idea cards
JSX component composition with small helper components
concerns: `mood` and `ideas` are hardcoded, not driven by `project` or data
No guard for missing `anchors[0]?.src` when rendering the hero image
Empty `alt` attribute on the mood image hurts accessibility
Heavy reliance on `window.*` globals makes the component fragile outside its runtime
No prop-types or TypeScript types for `project` or other props
notes: This is the project's creative starting board: it surfaces intent, references, and mood, and explicitly shows what context will seed later generation.
questions: What is the exact shape of `project` and `data.mockups`?
Is the hardcoded `mood` a placeholder for wizard-selected data?
How is `style_anchor` status assigned to mockups?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a warm intent-and-reference board; actual rendering matches that shape, but the mood and ideas are hardcoded rather than editable or data-driven, so it reads more like a scaffolded prototype than a live board.
apparent_themes: Context persistence and traceability across workspaces
intention_signals: To create a trustworthy creative starting space where mood, references, and principles are gathered visibly so that downstream generation stays coherent and grounded.
novelty: The explicit 'carry forward' contract and the warm-wall metaphor as a design substrate, plus treating style anchors as persistent context rather than one-off inputs.
contribution: A workspace UI pattern that combines project concept, reference anchors, editable ideas, direction/mood palette, and a visible carry-forward contract, intended to keep downstream generation aligned with the project's intent.
summary_for_embedding: A React workspace component that renders the 'Vibe' project starting board—concept, style-anchor references, ideas, direction/mood palette, and a carry-forward contract—using a mix of global runtime data and hardcoded mood content.
intention_for_embedding: To give a project a warm, reference-grounded starting space whose mood, anchors, and rules automatically feed downstream generation.

symbols.does: Renders a compact row with an icon badge, a label, and a muted detail line.
symbols.description: CarryRow is a small presentational helper used in the 'What carries forward' panel. It displays a gold-toned icon badge, a primary label, and a secondary detail string, aligning the three horizontally with flex layout.
